hazard.summary: Provider Retry-After and rate-limit reset headers are overridden at the CLI ApiClient retry boundary by retryCount-based delay logic, causing rate-limited requests to retry immediately and enter a retry loop.
signals: HTTP-date format Retry-After was silently dropped via parseInt → NaN

constraints: Retry logic must read provider-supplied Retry-After, RateLimit-Reset, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers before computing retry delay.
A client-specific retry configuration must not replace a richer shared retry policy with one that ignores rate-limit timing signals.
Retry timing parsers must accept both delta-seconds and HTTP-date Retry-After formats before falling back to local backoff.
Rate-limit responses must not be retried with the same timing policy as generic transient server errors.
Fallback retry delay must not produce a zero-delay first retry.
verification: Mock a CLI ApiClient response with status 429 and Retry-After: 2. Invoke retry delay for the first attempt. Assert delay is 2000ms, not 0ms.
Mock a CLI ApiClient response with status 429 and Retry-After set to an HTTP-date string. Invoke retry delay. Assert delay equals the positive difference between the date and now.
Mock a CLI ApiClient response with status 429 and RateLimit-Reset header. Assert retry delay uses RateLimit-Reset before falling back to retryCount-based delay.
Mock a CLI ApiClient response with status 500 and no rate-limit headers. Assert fallback delay is at least 1000ms and not 0ms.
Route a CLI command through ApiClient to a mock server returning 429 with Retry-After: 1 then success. Assert the second request is delayed by approximately 1000ms.
notes: The CLI retry implementation in packages/cli/src/api/retry.ts overrides provider retry timing signals with degraded retryCount-based delay logic despite a correct implementation existing in packages/sdk/src/retry.ts in the same repo. The SDK reads Retry-After, RateLimit-Reset, and X-RateLimit-Reset and parses both seconds and HTTP-date formats. The CLI passes its degraded config into all API calls via new client.Client({ retry: retry.config }) in packages/cli/src/api/client.ts. Secondary issue: HTTP-date Retry-After was silently dropped via parseInt returning NaN — fixed in a second commit after Greptile code review identified the gap. PR merged same day by engineering team lead.

constraints: Provider retry timing headers must propagate into retry delay decisions.
Retry-After parsing must support both seconds and HTTP-date formats.
Fallback backoff must not produce zero-delay initial retries.
Rate-limit and 5xx retry paths must not share identical timing policy.
anti_patterns: zero-ms first retry on rate-limited responses
treating 429 and 5xx identically in retry timing
parseInt on HTTP-date Retry-After returning NaN
duplicating SDK retry logic with degraded CLI version
